The deal involves equity and warrant subscriptions, equity purchases, and an open offer.

articles.headline: Nifty recovers from gap-down, energy stocks lead recovery
articles.summary: Equity benchmarks recovered on Wednesday, with Nifty 50 closing up 41 points at 23,659. Buying in energy, auto, and realty stocks offset rupee weakness and high crude prices. Oil & Gas rallied over 1.5%, driven by IOC, BPCL, and Reliance Industries, reacting to domestic fuel price hikes and easing Brent crude.

articles.headline: Aditya Birla Capital to raise ₹4,000 crore via preferential issue
articles.summary: Aditya Birla Capital (ABCL) plans to raise ₹4,000 crore through a preferential issue, including ₹2,880 crore from Grasim Industries and ₹920 crore from IFC. The capital infusion will strengthen its base, fund lending, and support growth, particularly for MSME business loans and digital-first solutions.

articles.headline: HDFC Mutual reports cybersecurity threat; no material impact expected
articles.summary: HDFC Asset Management Company reported a cybersecurity threat on May 16, with an anonymous source claiming access to IT infrastructure. The company activated containment protocols and engaged a specialist firm. Initial assessment suggests no material impact on operations or business continuity.

articles.headline: Wealth Company Large & Mid Cap Fund NFO opens today
articles.summary: The Wealth Company Mutual Fund has launched its Large & Mid Cap Fund NFO today, closing June 4. The open-ended equity scheme will invest in both large and mid-cap stocks, maintaining a minimum 35% allocation to each for long-term capital appreciation through an actively managed portfolio.

articles.headline: SMR Jewels IPO opens May 26, targets ₹67.23 crore
articles.summary: Ahmedabad-based SMR Jewels will launch its IPO on the BSE-SME platform on May 26, targeting ₹67.23 crore. The issue, priced at ₹128-135 per share, comprises a fresh issue of ₹54 crore and an OFS of ₹13.23 crore. Proceeds will fund a jewellery studio, debt repayment, and working capital.

articles.headline: PM Modi discusses trade, AI, energy, tech ties with Italian President
articles.summary: PM Narendra Modi met Italian President Sergio Mattarella to discuss expanding bilateral partnership in trade, investment, technology, critical minerals, AI, space, and nuclear energy. They emphasized the early signing of the India-EU Free Trade Agreement and the significance of the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEC).

articles.headline: Greece’s Eurobank sets up India office to tap trade opportunities
articles.summary: Greece’s Eurobank, with €110 billion in assets, opened its first office outside Europe in India. The Mumbai office will advise on trade and investment opportunities, with an eye on the India-EU FTA. It also offers cross-border remittances for Indians in Greece via UPI.

articles.headline: PFC's 30-year, ₹26,000-cr loan sets nuclear financing benchmark
articles.summary: Stakeholders suggest a 30-year tenure loan facility for nuclear power plants (NPPs) to establish a sector-specific long-term financing mechanism. PFC's ₹26,000-crore loan to NPCIL is the first such in India, setting a benchmark. The approach combines government infrastructure bonds, DFI lending, and sovereign credit support.

articles.headline: Global rice prices poised to gain on supply woes, deficit
articles.summary: Global rice prices are expected to rise due to a supply deficit, declining output (projected 5mt drop in 2026-27), and increased consumption. Geopolitical concerns and weather risks further underpin the upward momentum. India, Myanmar, and the US face the largest production cuts, while China's stocks are forecast to increase.

articles.headline: Crude oil slumps after Trump signals end to Iran war
articles.summary: Crude oil prices fell over 2% on Wednesday as US President Donald Trump signaled the Iran war would end "very quickly." Brent crude futures dropped to $108.70 a barrel, and US West Texas Intermediate futures were down to $102.08, though investors remain wary of peace talks' outcome.

articles.headline: Higher global prices, tighter imports to boost India's oilseed output
articles.summary: Higher global edible oil prices and tighter import supplies are creating positive momentum for India's domestic oilseed sector, particularly mustard. The Solvent Extractors’ Association of India expects sustained price realisations and market sentiment to encourage farmers to expand oilseed acreage during the upcoming kharif season.

articles.headline: Arabica coffee slips to near 1-1/2 year low
articles.summary: Arabica coffee futures fell to near a 1-1/2 year low as top grower Brazil's harvest began, while raw sugar gained. Arabica coffee on ICE fell 1.5% to $2.5835 per lb, driven by prospects of a bumper crop in Brazil. White sugar rose 1.2% to $446.40 a tonne.

articles.headline: Copper ticks higher on Chile supply worries
articles.summary: Copper prices rose due to hopes for an end to the Iran war and after top producer Chile cut its production outlook. Benchmark three-month copper on the LME gained 1.1% to $13,559 a tonne. Nickel rose 0.4% to $18,880, and tin prices jumped 4.3% to $53,850.

articles.headline: IIL launches Granuvia insecticide for paddy crop pest control
articles.summary: Insecticides (India) Ltd (IIL) launched Granuvia, a new insecticide for paddy crops. Applied 30-35 days after transplanting, Granuvia provides up to 60 days of control against brown plant hoppers and helps manage stem borers, which cause significant yield losses.

articles.headline: Maharashtra tops FY26 vehicle sales; UP leads two-wheelers
articles.summary: Maharashtra recorded the highest passenger vehicle sales in FY26 (5.64 lakh units, 12.1% market share), while Uttar Pradesh led two-wheeler sales (31.8 lakh units, 14.7% share). PV growth was driven by GST rate reduction, personal income tax relief, and lower financing costs due to RBI repo rate cuts.

articles.headline: Kanan Devan Hills Plantations announces leadership changes
articles.summary: The Board of Directors of Kanan Devan Hills Plantations Co Pvt Ltd appointed K Mathew Abraham as Chairman and Managing Director, and Amir Abbas Khan as Executive Director and Chief Executive Officer.

articles.headline: CCI must use digital tech for competition watchdog role: C&AG
articles.summary: Comptroller & Auditor General (C&AG) K Sanjay Murthy urged the Competition Commission of India (CCI) to leverage digital market intelligence and AI/ML for its competition watchdog role. Proposed initiatives include secure analytical interfaces with Digital Public Infrastructure and building analytical prowess to assess GST systems, FASTag, and procurement datasets.

articles.headline: India eyes S. Korea's military industry for defence self-reliance
articles.summary: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh urged South Korea to jointly develop, produce, and export advanced defence technologies with India. During his visit, he chaired an industry roundtable, inviting Korean firms to strengthen engagement for "aatmanirbharta" (self-reliance) and contribute to long-term mutually beneficial collaboration, including missile sub-systems and radar.

articles.headline: Leather exports dip 1.6% amid US tariffs, W. Asia crisis
articles.summary: India's leather, leather products, and footwear exports declined 1.6% in dollar terms to $4.75 billion in FY26. Softer global demand, higher US reciprocal tariffs (later replaced by a 10% surcharge), and shipping disruptions from the West Asia crisis impacted shipments, though rupee-term exports rose 2.62%.

articles.headline: India Staffing Federation renews pitch for GST rationalisation
articles.summary: The India Staffing Federation (ISF) is renewing its call for a GST cut on outsourced manpower to 5% from 18% to incentivise formal hiring. This would expand payroll participation, improve financial inclusion, and boost economic activity, offsetting tax losses. The ISF has received support from some state governments.

articles.headline: Mills body urges TN CM to resolve power issues, freeze demand charges
articles.summary: The Southern India Mills Association (SIMA) urged Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay to resolve power-related issues affecting their units. They requested withdrawal of network charges for rooftop solar power, a freeze on demand charges at ₹608 per kVA for three years, and speedy implementation of textile schemes.

articles.headline: Mythos AI can weaponise minor bugs into major cyberattacks: Cloudflare
articles.summary: Cloudflare's study on Anthropic’s ‘Mythos Preview’ AI reveals its ability to autonomously chain minor software bugs into devastating exploits. The AI mimics senior researchers in combining attack primitives, effectively automating complex cyberattacks, despite erratic internal safety boundaries. This raises concerns about potential misuse, warning that the same capabilities could accelerate internet attacks.

articles.headline: L&T's Vyoma partners Open Dhi for secure cloud solutions
articles.summary: Larsen & Toubro’s sovereign AI cloud unit, Vyoma, partnered with Open Dhi Group to host the startup’s enterprise and retail technology platforms on India-based cloud infrastructure. The collaboration aims to offer enterprises a unified digital stack with guaranteed data residency within India, addressing growing regulatory and security concerns around cross-border data flows.

articles.headline: Dr Reddy's launches oral semaglutide biosimilar for type 2 diabetes
articles.summary: Dr Reddy’s Laboratories launched its oral semaglutide biosimilar, Obeda, for type 2 diabetes mellitus in India. Available in 3mg, 7mg, and 14mg strengths (priced at ₹99, ₹135, ₹225 per tablet respectively), this follows their generic semaglutide injection, expanding their GLP-1 portfolio for advanced diabetes care.

articles.headline: RBI revises loan recovery norms, curbs phone disabling tools
articles.summary: The RBI proposed strict curbs on lenders disabling mobile phone features for loan recovery, allowing it only after 90 days overdue, multiple notices, and a cure period. Lenders are barred from accessing or retaining borrower data and must maintain call records of recovery agents.

articles.headline: Rupee touches fresh low, nears 97 a dollar
articles.summary: The Indian rupee hit a new low of 96.83 against the dollar due to elevated crude oil prices, rising US treasury yields, and global risk aversion, depreciating over 6% since the West Asia conflict began. RBI intervened at 96.96 and announced a $5 billion buy-sell swap auction to inject liquidity.

articles.headline: IRB Infrastructure Developers Q4FY26 net profit up 38%
articles.summary: IRB Infrastructure Developers' Q4FY26 net profit rose by 38% to ₹296 crore.

articles.headline: Eris Lifesciences Q4FY26 net more than doubled
articles.summary: Eris Lifesciences reported its Q4FY26 net profit more than doubled, reaching ₹282 crore.

articles.headline: IOL Chemicals and Pharmaceuticals Q4FY26 net profit up 69%
articles.summary: IOL Chemicals and Pharmaceuticals recorded a 69% increase in Q4FY26 net profit, totaling ₹53 crore.

articles.headline: Refex Industries bags ~20 crore logistics order
articles.summary: Refex Industries secured a ₹20 crore order for logistics services from a miniratna company.

articles.headline: Nykaa seeks to include Meta in copyright fight over music
articles.summary: Nykaa asked a Delhi court to make Meta a party to a copyright dispute filed by Zee Entertainment, which is suing Nykaa for $210,000 for allegedly using its copyrighted songs in Instagram reels.

articles.headline: IAN group leads Anscer Robotics’ funding with ~45 cr
articles.summary: Deeptech startup Anscer Robotics secured ₹45 crore in Series A funding led by IAN Group, with participation from Info Edge and other angel investors. The Bengaluru-based company produces robots for manufacturing and warehouses.

articles.headline: Piper Serica launches ~800 crore Bharat Tech Fund
articles.summary: Mumbai-based asset manager Piper Serica launched the Bharat Tech Fund with a corpus of ₹800 crore to invest in semiconductor, defence, AI, deep tech, and space tech startups in Series A and B rounds.

articles.headline: Icra projects up to 5% growth in 2W sales
articles.summary: India's two-wheeler industry is projected to see moderate wholesale volume growth of 3-5% year-on-year in FY27, driven by regulatory reforms and healthy replacement cycles. Electric two-wheelers saw 68% retail growth in April, reaching 8% penetration.

articles.headline: CloudExtel plans up to ~2,000 cr acquisition fund
articles.summary: Macquarie-backed CloudExtel plans to spend ₹1,500-2,000 crore over 3-5 years to acquire smaller cable operating companies and niche players, aiming for a larger share of the network-as-a-service (NaaS) market. It clocked revenues of ₹224 crore in FY25.

articles.headline: Bhargava puts biogas ahead of hybrids
articles.summary: Maruti Suzuki Chairman R C Bhargava advocates for greater government incentives for biogas-powered vehicles over hybrids, citing net-zero carbon emissions and organic manure production. He argues for GST differentiation: lower for EVs, higher for hybrids than EVs but lower than petrol/diesel.

articles.headline: Fake citations in Essel insolvency case under review: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court is reviewing concerns about allegedly fake judicial precedents used by NCLT and NCLAT in affirming Essel Infraprojects' insolvency case. The bench stated administrative review is underway and the matter has been referred to a committee, while reserving verdict on an appeal.

articles.headline: Punjab tops FY26 PV sales growth, Gujarat in two-wheelers
articles.summary: Punjab recorded the highest passenger vehicle (PV) sales growth in FY26 at 16.29% Y-o-Y, with 181,750 units sold. Gujarat led in two-wheeler sales growth, increasing 21.57% Y-o-Y to 1.569 million units in FY26.

articles.headline: India’s wellness drug market catching GenZ in the mirror
articles.summary: Internet-savvy GenZ consumers, representing 26% of India's population, are driving demand for wellness, appearance, and lifestyle-linked therapies. This cohort's proactive, self-directed healthcare approach boosts sales of anti-obesity, ophthalmology, dermatology, and oral care drugs, influenced by screen time and social media.

articles.headline: FMCG likely to see 4.5% volume growth in 2026
articles.summary: Fast-moving consumer goods (FMCG) companies expect 4.5-5% volume growth in 2026, assuming stable energy prices and normal monsoon. Household care and personal care segments are projected to lead growth, while food products could see 3-4% growth. Higher energy costs could temper growth.

articles.headline: Kalpataru Projects eyes ~30K cr orders in FY27
articles.summary: Kalpataru Projects International Limited (KPIL) targets ₹30,000 crore in order inflow for FY27, boosting its order book to ₹65,000-70,000 crore. The EPC company is expanding into solar EPC, especially in West Asia, and plans to enter the nuclear power sector.

articles.headline: Tata Power EV Charging Solutions expands network
articles.summary: Tata Power EV Charging Solutions expanded its ultra-fast EV charging network on the Delhi-Mumbai Expressway by establishing stations with 240 kW chargers and four guns, in collaboration with Indian Oil Corp.

articles.headline: NephroPlus sharpens global push as biz grows
articles.summary: NephroPlus, Asia's only listed dialysis company, is transforming into a global network, operating over 520 clinics across India, Philippines, Saudi Arabia, Uzbekistan, and Nepal. International business grew to 42% of revenue in FY26, driven by acquisitions and government PPPs.

articles.headline: India’s RE push may unlock up to $15 bn land investments by ’30
articles.summary: India's renewable energy (RE) expansion, targeting 500 GW non-fossil fuel capacity by 2030, is expected to drive $10-15 billion in land investments by 2030. This is based on $110-120 billion projected RE sector investments.

articles.headline: Assam to pass UCC legislation in first Assembly session
articles.summary: Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma announced his government's plan to introduce and pass legislation on the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) during the upcoming four-day Assembly session (May 21-26).

articles.headline: Nationwide one-day chemist strike gets lukewarm response
articles.summary: A one-day strike called by the All India Organisation of Chemists and Druggists (AIOCD) against unregulated online pharmacies saw a lukewarm response. AIOCD demands action against "illegal" deep discounting practices by online players.

articles.headline: Skydo gets GIFT City nod to expand global payment services
articles.summary: Cross-border payments fintech Skydo received in-principle approval to operate as a payment service provider at GIFT City IFSC, enabling expansion of multi-currency collections, e-money accounts, and merchant acquisition, while building new payment corridors.

articles.headline: Sitharaman discusses deepening India-US partnership with Gor
articles.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman and Minister Jitendra Singh met US Ambassador Sergio Gor to discuss deepening India-US fintech collaboration, investment, and nuclear sector cooperation ahead of Secretary of State Marco Rubio's visit.

articles.headline: OIL eyes global assets to strengthen its energy play
articles.summary: State-run Oil India Limited (OIL) is exploring overseas asset acquisitions in Latin America, Canada, Australia, and Africa to diversify energy sources amid West Asia crisis. OIL aims to increase domestic crude oil production to 4 million tonnes in FY27 from 3.5 million tonnes.

articles.headline: Jubilant net profit up 67% to ~82.42 cr
articles.summary: Quick service restaurant chain operator Jubilant FoodWorks reported a 67% rise in consolidated net profit to ₹82.42 crore for Q4FY26, with revenue at ₹2,499.46 crore. Temporary LPG supply constraints impacted like-for-like growth.

articles.headline: Gold loans drove FY26 retail credit growth
articles.summary: Gold loans emerged as the fastest-growing retail credit segment in FY26, surging 50.4% Y-o-Y to ₹18.6 trillion, surpassing personal loans. Rising bullion prices and an increased loan-to-value cap boosted growth. Overall retail loans rose 16.6% to ₹170.2 trillion.

articles.headline: Eurobank to open India representative office
articles.summary: Athens-headquartered Eurobank will open its first India representative office and activate cross-border remittances from Greece to India via UPI, aiming to strengthen trade/investment and position Greece/Cyprus as entry points for Indian companies in the EU.

articles.headline: Axis Pension Fund eyes 500K new subscribers, ~30K cr AUM in FY27
articles.summary: Axis Pension Fund aims to add 500,000 subscribers and grow its AUM to ₹30,000 crore in FY27, leveraging digital onboarding via platforms like Google Pay and PhonePe, and new products like NPS Vatsalya, to expand pension penetration.

articles.headline: Rajasthan: 260K farmers to get subsidised seeds
articles.summary: Rajasthan's government will provide subsidised seeds for pulse and oilseed crops to 260,000 farmers in the current season, with an expenditure of ₹135 crore under the 'National Mission on Edible Oils.' This aims to boost production and farmer incomes.

articles.headline: India’s submarine gap with rivals widens
articles.summary: India inducted the nuclear-powered INS Aridhaman, bolstering its SSBN arm. However, its conventional diesel-electric submarine fleet faces challenges, with Pakistan operating AIP-equipped submarines. Experts warn India's fleet is inadequate, urging faster procurement under its 30-year submarine construction program.

articles.headline: Food industry to promote startups, cloud kitchens
articles.summary: Industry bodies plan to promote food startups and cloud kitchens in Uttar Pradesh, leveraging the state's agricultural strength and supportive investment policy. This initiative aligns with the government's One District, One Cuisine (ODOC) scheme to gain national and global recognition for dishes.

articles.headline: India, Italy to increase bilateral trade to €20 billion by 2029
articles.summary: PM Narendra Modi and Italian PM Giorgia Meloni committed to increasing India-Italy bilateral trade to €20 billion by 2029. Outcomes include defence industrial roadmap, critical minerals collaboration, mobility pact for Indian nurses, and promoting marine exports. Meloni thanked Modi for 'Melody' toffees.

articles.headline: NITI Aayog paper proposes state-run migration systems
articles.summary: A NITI Aayog paper suggests Indian states establish dedicated state-run migration systems, including recruitment agencies and language training, to treat overseas employment as an economic development strategy. It recommends digital platforms to track global labor demand and integrate with national systems.

articles.headline: More judges in SC, but backlog mounting
articles.summary: President Droupadi Murmu increased the Supreme Court's sanctioned strength from 33 to 37 judges, excluding the CJI, amid mounting case backlogs. Despite expansion over decades, pending cases have risen, with 43% pending for three years or more, highlighting persistent challenges in caseload management.

articles.headline: Crack down on Neet-UG misinformation networks: Govt
articles.summary: The government asked Meta, Google, and Telegram to intensify monitoring and take down networks spreading misinformation about the National Eligibility cum Entrance Test (Undergraduate) (Neet-UG) re-examination. This follows Neet-UG 2026 cancellation due to paper leak allegations.

articles.headline: Odisha approves new law for marine fishing sector
articles.summary: The Odisha cabinet approved the Odisha Marine Fishing Regulation Bill, 2026, replacing a four-decade-old Act. The new law aims to boost deep-sea fishing, seafood exports, coastal security, marine conservation, and sustainable resource management, promoting economically viable marine activities.

articles.headline: Ebola likely circulating in Congo for 2 months
articles.summary: The WHO reported that the Ebola outbreak in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo, linked to over 130 deaths, likely began two months ago and is expected to grow. The rare Bundibugyo strain, lacking a vaccine, went undetected in a densely populated area.

articles.headline: New UAE oil pipeline bypassing Hormuz now 50% complete
articles.summary: The UAE's new crude pipeline bypassing the Strait of Hormuz is 50% complete, aiming to double Fujairah export capacity by 2027. ADNOC's head stated global oil flows might take at least four months to recover after the Iran war ends.

articles.headline: OpenAI commits $234 mn for 1st applied AI lab outside US
articles.summary: OpenAI will open its first applied AI lab outside the US in Singapore, committing over S$300 million ($235 million). The lab will staff around 200 roles in coming years, collaborating with Singapore to advance AI innovation and accessibility.

articles.headline: Early war goal was to install hardline former President as Iran’s leader
articles.summary: US and Israel initially planned to install hardline former Iranian President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad as Iran’s leader after Israeli strikes killed top officials. Ahmadinejad, injured in a strike, became disillusioned with the regime change plan.

articles.headline: China confirms it will buy 200 Boeing jets
articles.summary: China's commerce ministry confirmed a purchase of 200 Boeing jets, signaling a desire to stabilize economic and trade ties with the US, independent of its commitments with Russia.

articles.headline: Samsung reaches tentative union deal, averting crippling strike
articles.summary: Samsung Electronics Co reached a tentative wage agreement with its labor union, averting a crippling strike at the world’s largest memory chipmaker. This deal heads off production disruptions and aids development of next-generation semiconductors.

articles.headline: Indonesia unveils plan to centralise control of commodity exports
articles.summary: Indonesia plans to centralise exports of key commodities like palm oil and coal through its sovereign wealth fund, Danantara, aiming to boost government revenue. This move, starting with palm oil, coal, and ferroalloys, tackles under-invoicing and transfer pricing concerns.

articles.headline: StanChart CEO reassures staff over ‘lower value human capital’ comment
articles.summary: Standard Chartered CEO Bill Winters reassured staff after remarks about replacing "lower-value human capital" with technology, following the bank's announcement to cut 15% of corporate function roles (around 8,000 jobs) by 2030, driven by AI.

articles.headline: Wipro’s long wait for turnaround
articles.summary: Wipro is undergoing a prolonged turnaround, with CEO Srini Pallia focusing on large deals and AI-native, platform-led services to boost revenue. While its large deal bookings reached $7.8 billion in FY26, the company still lags behind competitors like HCLTech, facing challenges in converting consulting wins.

articles.headline: What changes with Google’s ‘AI Search’
articles.summary: Google is revamping Search with AI at its core, moving from links to direct answers via an "Intelligent Search Box" and generative user interface. AI Overviews and AI Mode have crossed billions of users. The shift also includes "agentic Search experiences" for continuous information monitoring.

articles.headline: Cybercriminals promoted illegal betting platforms during IPL: CloudSEK
articles.summary: Cybercriminals promoted illegal betting platforms on over 1,200 domains, including government websites, during IPL 2026, using AI deepfake tools to fabricate endorsements by Indian cricketers. CloudSEK found one platform operating over 25 betting websites from a single backend.

articles.headline: India records all-time high power demand of 265 Gw
articles.summary: India's peak power demand hit a new record of 265.44 GW on Wednesday, for the third consecutive day, amidst orange alerts for severe heatwave conditions across parts of the country, including Delhi, which recorded 45.8 degrees Celsius.

articles.headline: SC seeks Centre’s response on PIL demanding 30% quota for women lawyers
articles.summary: The Supreme Court sought responses from the Centre and states on a PIL seeking a minimum 30% quota for women lawyers in government panels and law officer positions. The plea highlights systematic blocking of women from professional advancement despite high law school enrollment.

articles.headline: We still lack a robust climate risk hedging tool: NCDEX chief
articles.summary: NCDEX launched India’s first exchange-traded weather derivatives contract, Rain Mumbai, to hedge monsoon risks for agriculture and related sectors. MD & CEO Arun Raste aims to expand into non-agri commodities and equities, seeking to become a multi-segment exchange and a price discovery center for underserved products.

articles.headline: OMC stocks see some rebound, but outlook still hazy
articles.summary: Oil marketing companies (OMCs) reported good Q4FY26 results, but Q1FY27 outlook is hazy due to high crude/gas prices from the West Asia conflict. Price hikes of ~₹3.90/litre offer partial relief, but LPG losses widened. Analysts expect earnings downgrades if prices remain elevated.

articles.headline: Mkts rebound; Sensex climbs 117 points on fag-end buying
articles.summary: Benchmark equity indices rebounded on Wednesday, with the BSE Sensex climbing 117.54 points (0.16%) to 75,318.39 and Nifty gaining 41 points (0.17%) to 23,659. Rally in Reliance Industries and fag-end buying offset rupee weakness and elevated crude prices. FIIs offloaded ₹2,457.49 crore equities.

articles.headline: Edelweiss Mutual Fund unveils second offering Altiva SIF
articles.summary: Edelweiss Mutual Fund launched Altiva Equity Ex-Top 100 Long-Short Fund, its second specialized investment fund (SIF) under Altiva SIF. It offers investors access to underserved market segments through a long-short strategy.

articles.headline: Modi’s toffee gift to Meloni sparks rally in wrong stock
articles.summary: Shares of Parle Industries surged 5% after Prime Minister Narendra Modi gifted Italian counterpart Giorgia Meloni "Melody" toffees. Traders seemingly mistook the software company for privately-held Parle Products, the actual maker of the candies.

articles.headline: Consumption stocks face fuel price impact
articles.summary: Consumption stocks may face pressure as rising fuel prices lead to higher input costs for companies. Amul and Mother Dairy already increased milk prices. A prolonged high-inflation environment, potentially exacerbated by deficient monsoon rainfall, could hurt demand and profit margins.

articles.headline: India-Italy ties hit sweet spot: Deals on def, critical minerals
articles.summary: PM Modi and PM Meloni met at the G7 summit in Italy. They agreed to a strategic partnership roadmap focused on defense, clean energy, and critical minerals. A new deal on critical minerals was launched to strengthen supply chains and cooperation in mobility and migration.

articles.headline: Will ask for CBI probe into Twisha case, says MP CM
articles.summary: Madhya Pradesh CM Mohan Yadav stated he would request a CBI inquiry into the death of Twisha Sharma in Noida. A local court previously rejected a plea for a second autopsy. Twisha was found dead under suspicious circumstances, and her family has alleged harassment by her in-laws for dowry.

articles.headline: SC: Animal lovers can protect strays if they take responsibility
articles.summary: The Supreme Court observed that while animal activists can feed and protect stray dogs, they must ensure such actions do not harm others. The court emphasized that the right to protect dogs must be balanced with responsibility for public safety following incidents of stray dog bites.

articles.headline: Row erupts over Commonwealth short story award to Caribbean writer
articles.summary: A controversy emerged after Hanaa Nazir won the Commonwealth Short Story Prize. Critics and AI detection tools suggested the story might be AI-generated. The Commonwealth Foundation defended the decision, stating they used human reviewers and reliable software to verify the work.

articles.headline: 26-year search by 3 families ends as van found in canal
articles.summary: Three families in Patiala ended a 26-year search for missing loved ones who vanished in 2000. A submerged Maruti van was discovered in the Bhakra canal containing human remains, a school uniform, and personal belongings, providing closure for the relatives.

articles.headline: KG Basin: Reliance Industries wants to sort dispute with Centre
articles.summary: Reliance Industries informed the Supreme Court of its intention to settle a long-standing dispute with the Indian government regarding the KG-D6 gas field. The dispute involves a $2.8 billion claim. The court has allowed both parties time to reach an amicable solution.

articles.headline: Basic amenities missing at Delhi slum resettlement hub
articles.summary: Residents of the Savda Ghevra resettlement colony in Delhi report a severe lack of basic services like clean water, electricity, and security. Despite being relocated there from various slums years ago, the infrastructure remains incomplete, causing hardship during heatwaves.

articles.headline: NEET re-test: Education ministry implements 20-day security overhaul
articles.summary: Ahead of the NEET-UG re-examination, the education ministry is implementing a 20-day security overhaul. Measures include stricter candidate verification, enhanced center surveillance, and new protocols for handling question papers to prevent leaks and ensure transparency.

articles.headline: Two juveniles stabbed to death by minor in Delhi's Rohini
articles.summary: Two minors were allegedly stabbed to death by a 14-year-old boy in Delhi's Rohini area following a dispute. The accused was apprehended by police. The incident occurred while the victims were playing in a park and an argument turned violent.

articles.headline: City heatwave alert extended till May 26 as temperatures soar
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department has extended the heatwave alert for Delhi until May 26. Temperatures in some parts reached 46.5 degrees Celsius. An orange alert remains in place, and residents are advised to take precautions against heat-related illnesses.

articles.headline: Remarks against judiciary land YouTuber in jail for six months
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court sentenced a YouTuber to six months in jail for contempt of court. The individual had posted videos with derogatory remarks against judges and the judicial system. The court noted that the person showed no remorse and had committed a similar offense before.

articles.headline: Masala flagged as drugs, businessman spends 57 days in jail
articles.summary: A businessman in Bhopal spent 57 days in jail after regular spices he was carrying were flagged as drugs by an explosive detection machine. Forensic reports later confirmed the mistake. The High Court awarded him ₹10 lakh in compensation for the ordeal.

articles.headline: Why Kamla Nehru Ridge is the hottest area in Delhi
articles.summary: Kamla Nehru Ridge has been recording the highest temperatures in Delhi despite its greenery. Experts attribute this to its rocky terrain that traps heat, ecological degradation, and the presence of invasive species like Prosopis juliflora that disrupt natural cooling mechanisms.

articles.headline: Kavach 4.0 automatic safety system implemented on 86km rail track
articles.summary: Indian Railways has successfully implemented the Kavach 4.0 automatic train protection system on an 86km stretch between New Delhi and Palwal. The system prevents collisions and signal passing at danger, enhancing safety on one of the busiest rail sections.

articles.headline: National Green Tribunal: Ridge Management Board must continue protection work
articles.summary: The National Green Tribunal has directed the Delhi Ridge Management Board to continue its work of protecting the ridge area. The tribunal emphasized that the board must fulfill its statutory duties despite delays in appointing permanent members from civil society.

articles.headline: Murder convict nabbed after 24 years living under false identity
articles.summary: A murder convict who jumped parole in 2002 was arrested in Prayagraj after 24 years on the run. Rajesh Patel had been living under a false name as a businessman. He was originally convicted for a 1990 murder in Delhi and had eluded authorities by changing locations.

articles.headline: CBSE Class XII students face technical glitches on re-evaluation website
articles.summary: Several Class XII students faced difficulties accessing the CBSE website for mark verification and re-evaluation. Technical glitches led to delays in submitting applications, prompting CBSE to extend the deadline. Officials confirmed that the issues were being resolved.

articles.headline: Delhi government launches talent hunt program with prizes for youth
articles.summary: The Delhi government has launched 'Haushon Ki Udaan 2024-27,' a talent hunt program to identify and promote young talent in fields like singing, dancing, and theatre. Winners can receive cash prizes of up to ₹2.5 lakh and opportunities for professional training.

articles.headline: High Court directs Delhi government to frame SOP for poor patients
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court has directed the city government to frame a Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) to ensure that poor patients receive free treatment at private hospitals. The move aims to streamline access to healthcare for eligible individuals under existing policies.

articles.headline: Students protest cockroach found in Delhi University hostel mess food
articles.summary: Students at a Delhi University South Campus hostel held protests after a cockroach was allegedly found in food served at the mess. The incident sparked concerns over hygiene and food safety. University authorities have promised to investigate and take corrective measures.

articles.headline: New AI HoloBox brings Mahatma Gandhi to life for visitors
articles.summary: An AI-powered HoloBox featuring a 3D digital avatar of Mahatma Gandhi has been unveiled at the Pradhanmantri Sangrahalaya in Delhi. Visitors can interact with the avatar and ask questions about his life and philosophy, providing an immersive educational experience.

articles.headline: Rape accused gets bail and garlanded welcome in Ghaziabad
articles.summary: A man accused of rape was released on bail and welcomed home with a car convoy and garlands in Ghaziabad. The incident has drawn criticism from activists and the victim's family, who stated that such displays are used to intimidate survivors.

articles.headline: Dindori district in Madhya Pradesh achieves success in water conservation
articles.summary: The Dindori district in Madhya Pradesh has seen significant success in water conservation through community-led initiatives. Thousands of rainwater harvesting structures and check dams have been built, helping to recharge groundwater and improve local agriculture.

articles.headline: Air quality in Srinagar, Uttarakhand, drops to very poor level
articles.summary: Air quality in Srinagar, Uttarakhand, reached a 'very poor' level with an AQI of 390. Officials attribute the spike to forest fires in the region and stagnant weather conditions. Residents are advised to take precautions against respiratory issues.

articles.headline: Hyderabad police arrest 66 for illegally supplying SIM cards for fraud
articles.summary: Hyderabad police arrested 66 individuals for allegedly supplying thousands of illegally obtained SIM cards used in cyber frauds. The cards were linked to 76 cases across India involving ₹101 crore. Police seized equipment used to activate the SIMs.

articles.headline: Ladakh hospitality sector granted industry status to boost tourism
articles.summary: The Ladakh administration has granted 'industry' status to the hospitality sector in the union territory. This move allows hotels and guesthouses to avail benefits like lower electricity rates and subsidies, aiming to encourage investment and boost local tourism.

articles.headline: Four killed and several injured in Uttar Pradesh highway crash
articles.summary: Four people were killed and others injured when their vehicle crashed into a stationary truck on a highway in Uttar Pradesh. The victims were traveling from Bareilly to Varanasi. Police are investigating the cause, citing possible over-speeding.

articles.headline: Suvendu Adhikari: Migrants not covered by CAA will be deported
articles.summary: West Bengal BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari stated that migrants not covered under the Citizenship Amendment Act (CAA) will eventually be deported. He emphasized that the act provides citizenship to persecuted minorities while identifying illegal immigrants.

articles.headline: Censor board to develop protocol for films portraying intelligence agencies
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court directed the censor board to develop a protocol for reviewing films that portray intelligence agencies. The move follows a petition raising concerns that such movies could compromise national security by revealing sensitive information.

articles.headline: Bombay High Court: Wife failing to do chores is not cruelty
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court observed that a wife cannot be treated as a maid and her failure to perform household chores does not amount to cruelty under the law. The court made the observation while hearing a divorce petition filed by a husband on those grounds.

articles.headline: Health survey results ready for year but not made public
articles.summary: Results from the National Family Health Survey (NFHS-6) have reportedly been ready for over a year but are yet to be released by the government. Experts express concern that the delay could hinder effective healthcare planning and policy-making.

articles.headline: PM Modi receives FAO honor for successful agricultural schemes
articles.summary: PM Modi was honored by the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) for India's implementation of key agricultural and food security schemes. The award recognizes the country's efforts in achieving self-sufficiency in food production and supporting farmers.

articles.headline: RBI prohibits lenders from locking phones or shaming defaulting borrowers
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India issued new guidelines prohibiting lenders from remotely locking mobile phones of defaulting borrowers or using social media to shame them. The rules aim to protect consumer privacy and prevent coercive recovery practices.

articles.headline: KKR keep IPL playoff hopes alive with win over Mumbai Indians
articles.summary: Kolkata Knight Riders (KKR) kept their IPL playoff hopes alive with a victory over Mumbai Indians. Manish Pandey and Rovman Powell's crucial partnership helped KKR chase down the target on a challenging pitch at Eden Gardens.

articles.headline: Government decides against new hydroelectric projects on Ganga in Uttarakhand
articles.summary: The Indian government has decided not to allow any new hydroelectric projects on the Ganga river in Uttarakhand. The decision aims to protect the river's ecology and prevent environmental damage in the sensitive Himalayan region.

articles.headline: Israel moves toward early elections as Knesset votes to dissolve
articles.summary: Israel is closer to early elections after a preliminary vote in the Knesset to dissolve the parliament. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's coalition is facing internal pressure over conflict management and domestic policy issues.

articles.headline: Xi and Putin criticize US policy during meeting in Beijing
articles.summary: During a meeting in Beijing, Chinese President Xi Jinping and Russian President Vladimir Putin expressed a united front against US foreign policy. However, they failed to finalize a major gas pipeline deal that has been under negotiation.

articles.headline: Historical monuments in Delhi facing neglect and urban encroachment
articles.summary: Several historical monuments in Delhi are facing neglect and encroachment due to urban development and lack of maintenance. Experts warn that many lesser-known sites are at risk of being lost if immediate conservation efforts are not taken.

articles.headline: Missing and broken railings on Delhi roads pose safety risk
articles.summary: Large sections of railings on Delhi's bridges and roads are missing or broken, posing a safety risk to pedestrians and motorists. Officials attribute the problem to theft and lack of maintenance, calling for urgent repairs.

articles.headline: SC finds no issue with caste enumeration in Census 2027, dismisses plea.
articles.summary: The Supreme Court stated that ascertaining caste is a policy matter for the government, not its domain to decide if it should be part of Census 2027. It dismissed a plea against caste enumeration, which is included in the second phase of Census 2027. The last comprehensive nationwide caste Census was in 1931.

articles.headline: Delhi Police nab 89 in month-long cybercrime crackdown, recover ₹70 crore.
articles.summary: Delhi's West district Cyber Police arrested 35 and held 54 individuals in preventive custody during a month-long crackdown. The operation targeted digital arrests, investment frauds, fake dating apps, and mule accounts, recovering ₹70 crore and hundreds of SIM/ATM cards.

articles.headline: Delhi government launches ₹473-crore project to replace street lights with LEDs.
articles.summary: The Delhi government will replace nearly 96,000 street lights with energy-efficient LEDs at a cost of ₹473.24 crore before Deepavali. The project aims to improve safety, reduce accidents, save ₹25 crore annually in power, and provide better lighting quality.

articles.headline: Delhi CM launches ₹10-crore ‘Hauslon Ki Udaan’ talent hunt scheme.
articles.summary: Delhi CM Rekha Gupta launched the ‘Talent Hunt Scheme - Hauslon Ki Udaan 2026-27’ with a ₹10 crore outlay. It aims to identify and nurture young talent in seven categories, offering cash prizes up to ₹2.5 lakh for first place.

articles.headline: IIT Delhi mentors 120 government school girls in STEM.
articles.summary: IIT Delhi concluded its fifth 'Manasvi' STEM mentorship program, hosting 120 government school girls from Delhi-NCR. The initiative aims to encourage careers in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics through academic and leadership sessions, and hands-on experiments.

articles.headline: Man, brother arrested for dowry death in Delhi.
articles.summary: Delhi police arrested Raju Singh (27) and his younger brother Rajkumar (22) for the dowry death of Singh's wife, who died after falling from a building in Inderpuri. A case under Section 80 (dowry death) and 3(5) (common intention) of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita was registered.

articles.headline: MCD to explore euthanasia for aggressive stray dogs after SC ruling.
articles.summary: Following a Supreme Court ruling allowing euthanasia for "rabid, incurably ill or demonstrably dangerous or aggressive dogs," the MCD is considering implementing a policy. A meeting is scheduled to determine the course of action and infrastructure needs for managing stray dog populations.

articles.headline: Bengal Cabinet expansion uncertain 10 days post-BJP government swearing-in.
articles.summary: Ten days after the BJP government was sworn in West Bengal, the allocation of portfolios remains largely unclear. CM Suvendu Adhikari holds 42 of 54 departments. Speculation is rife regarding the expansion and who will get key roles, with the Budget Session starting June 18.

articles.headline: Amit Shah unveils development plan for 'Naxal-free' Bastar.
articles.summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah visited Chhattisgarh's Bastar region, declaring it "free from Naxalism" and outlining a development blueprint for the next five years. He announced the conversion of police camps into public service centers and stressed ideological fight against Maoism.

articles.headline: Husband's mother, uncle arrested in Greater Noida dowry death.
articles.summary: Police in Greater Noida arrested the mother and uncle of the victim's husband in a 24-year-old woman's alleged dowry death case. The woman died after falling from her matrimonial home terrace. Her family alleges murder over demands for an SUV and cash.

articles.headline: Congress leaders meet Mayawati, spark alliance speculation.
articles.summary: Congress leaders Rajendra Pal Gautam and Tanuj Punia attempted to meet BSP president Mayawati in Lucknow, but were turned away. The visit sparked speculation about an alliance, with Congress stating it's the responsibility of like-minded people to oust the BJP government.

articles.headline: Himanta Sarma: No politics in Zubeen Garg's name.
articles.summary: Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma stated that the Congress-led Opposition paid the price for "politicising" musician Zubeen Garg's death in the Assembly poll. He urged against politics in the name of Garg, who died last year.

articles.headline: Bengal government to 'detect, delete, deport' illegal infiltrators: CM Adhikari.
articles.summary: West Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari announced a "detect, delete, and deport" policy for illegal infiltrators not covered under the CAA. The State police will arrest them and hand them to BSF for deportation. He also handed over 27 km of land for border fencing.

articles.headline: EPS camp MLA denies DMK-AIADMK alliance pact claim.
articles.summary: AIADMK MLA O.S. Manian (EPS camp) denied Minister Aadhav Arjuna's claim that AIADMK and DMK tried to forge an alliance before government formation. Manian stated that AIADMK leader Palaniswami consistently criticized the DMK, leading to their electoral success.

articles.headline: NCP (SP) rules out merger with Sunetra Pawar-led NCP faction.
articles.summary: Maharashtra NCP (SP) chief Shashikant Shinde ruled out a merger with the ruling NCP faction led by Deputy CM Sunetra Pawar. He stated the focus is on strengthening the party and intensifying public agitations under the Maha Vikas Aghadi banner.

articles.headline: Gujarat government backs 'One Nation, One Election' proposal to JPC.
articles.summary: Gujarat CM Bhupendra Patel met the Joint Parliamentary Committee (JPC) examining simultaneous elections, expressing full support for 'One Nation, One Election'. The Opposition Congress criticized the proposal as a hidden agenda for centralizing power.

articles.headline: J&K school renamed after pony operator killed saving tourists in Pahalgam.
articles.summary: The Jammu and Kashmir government renamed Government High School Hapatnar to Shaheed Adil Memorial High School Hapatnar, honoring pony ride operator Adil Hussain Shah. Shah died protecting tourists during a terror attack in Pahalgam in April 2025.

articles.headline: Akhilesh Yadav alleges U.P. govt. misappropriating reservation quotas in recruitments.
articles.summary: SP president Akhilesh Yadav released a ‘Samajwadi Audit Report,’ alleging the BJP government in Uttar Pradesh is weakening the reservation system. He claimed over 11,514 reserved category seats across 22 examinations were unfilled, denying OBCs, SCs, and minorities their rightful share.

articles.headline: High Court dismisses plea to deregister AAP, ban Kejriwal.
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court dismissed a petition seeking to deregister the Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) and disqualify its chief Arvind Kejriwal and other leaders. The court found the plea lacked merit, stating the Election Commission lacks power to deregister a political party.

articles.headline: Maharashtra completes documentation of 23,000+ wetlands for legal protection.
articles.summary: The NCSCM has completed documenting and ground-truthing 23,415 wetlands in Maharashtra, enabling their formal notification under environmental protection laws. This move, spurred by legal interventions and Supreme Court directions, aims to prevent their destruction and encroachment.

articles.headline: Punjab temple defers 'untimely' Rath Yatra after Puri protest.
articles.summary: The Ferozepur district administration in Punjab informed Puri Jagannath Temple authorities that Rath Yatra planned for May 23 has been postponed. Puri temple officials protested the date as not conforming to the prescribed religious calendar and scriptural traditions.

articles.headline: Three police injured in Mumbai slum stone pelting during anti-encroachment drive.
articles.summary: Three police personnel were injured in Mumbai's Bandra East after slum residents allegedly pelted stones during an anti-encroachment drive to raze an "illegal" mosque. Ten people were detained. Western Railway is clearing 5,200 sq.m of encroached land.

articles.headline: TMC MLAs protest hawker evictions and post-election violence in Bengal.
articles.summary: Trinamool Congress (TMC) legislators staged a protest on the West Bengal Assembly premises against hawker evictions in anti-encroachment drives and alleged targeting of party workers post-election. They accused the BJP government of "oppression" and "bulldozers" actions.

articles.headline: Kerala Ministers' portfolios announced after internal wrangling; CM retains 35.
articles.summary: The UDF government in Kerala notified Cabinet portfolios after delays due to internal and inter-party differences. CM V.D. Satheesan will handle 35 portfolios, including Finance, Law, and Ports, indicating a concentration of power. Ramesh Chennithala received Home, Vigilance.

articles.headline: Elderly woman dies of primary amoebic meningoencephalitis in Kerala.
articles.summary: A 73-year-old woman in Kerala's Ernakulam district died from primary amoebic meningoencephalitis (PAM), a rare and lethal central nervous system infection. She is suspected to have contracted it after washing her face with canal water.

articles.headline: Owaisi accuses Rijiju of denying Muslims their rights.
articles.summary: Hyderabad MP Asaduddin Owaisi accused Union Minister Kiren Rijiju of attempting to deny Muslims their fundamental rights under Article 30 of the Constitution. This followed Rijiju's comparison of Parsi and Muslim populations, which Owaisi criticized as a "Minister Against Minorities" logic.

articles.headline: Andhra Pradesh, Telangana reel under intense heatwave; Piduguralla 47.6°C.
articles.summary: Large parts of Andhra Pradesh and Telangana experienced intense heatwave conditions, with Piduguralla recording 47.6°C. IMD warns extreme weather to persist until Sunday. Health Minister advises special care for vulnerable populations.

articles.headline: Karnataka bans open safari jeeps in reserves, replaces with closed buses.
articles.summary: Karnataka's Environment Minister banned open safari vehicles in tiger reserves and wildlife sanctuaries, mandating buses with iron meshes and shatterproof glasses. Ambulances must also be stationed near safari centers and elephant camps, following a tourist's death.

articles.headline: Kerala HC slams delay in microfinance fraud probe, sets deadline.
articles.summary: The Kerala High Court directed the probe team to conclude the investigation within a month in the microfinance fraud case against SNDP Yogam general secretary Vellappally Natesan and others. The court noted the probe had been in its "final stage" since September 2025.

articles.headline: Kerala approves ₹10 lakh aid for kin of fireworks blast victims.
articles.summary: The Kerala government sanctioned ₹10 lakh each from the CMDRF to families of two persons killed in a temporary firecracker storage unit explosion in Thripunithura in February 2024. The blast also damaged 312 houses.

articles.headline: Defeat of delimitation Bill is south India's loss: Nara Lokesh.
articles.summary: Andhra Pradesh Minister Nara Lokesh states the defeat of the Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill for delimitation is a "grave injustice" to south India, as it will lose Lok Sabha representation after the next Census. He discusses AP's economy, education reforms, and incentives for larger families.

articles.headline: Congress slams Centre over inflation, unemployment, economic distress.
articles.summary: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge criticized the Modi government over inflation, unemployment, and weakening economic indicators, accusing it of pursuing publicity over addressing public misery. He cited significant increases in petrol and diesel prices over 12 years.

articles.headline: Naga women's body petitions PM for release of six captives in Manipur.
articles.summary: An apex body of Naga women in Manipur petitioned PM Modi, seeking Centre's intervention for the release of six Naga men allegedly held hostage by Kuki groups. They also demanded abrogation of agreements with extremist groups and justice for a Naga villager killed.

articles.headline: SC "inclined" to grant bail to 2020 Delhi riots accused.
articles.summary: The Supreme Court indicated a prima facie inclination to grant bail to 2020 Delhi riots accused Tasleem Ahmed and Abdul Khalid Saifi, adjourning the hearing at Delhi Police's request. The case challenges the Delhi High Court's order denying them bail in the larger conspiracy case.

articles.headline: Norway condemns all international law violations, no double standards: FM Eide.
articles.summary: Norwegian Foreign Minister Espen Barth Eide asserts Norway condemns all violations of international law, including U.S.-Israel actions in Iran and U.S. operations in Venezuela, rejecting claims of double standards. He prefers India reduce Russian oil intake but understands energy security concerns.

articles.headline: Rahul Gandhi's 'traitor' remarks reflect 'anarchic mindset': BJP chief.
articles.summary: BJP president Nitin Nabin criticized Rahul Gandhi's "traitor" remark against PM Modi and Amit Shah, stating it reflects Gandhi's "anarchic mindset" and insults Indian citizens. Nabin warned of a "heavy price" for such comments, linking them to Gandhi's electoral defeats.

articles.headline: Left is only alternative to BJP's politics, to highlight livelihood crisis: CPI.
articles.summary: CPI general secretary D. Raja stated the Left is the only ideological alternative to the BJP’s aggressive right-wing politics. The CPI plans a nationwide campaign from August 6-15 to highlight livelihood crises, concluding with a Delhi rally in September.

articles.headline: Congress says no military component in Great Nicobar project.
articles.summary: Congress general secretary Jairam Ramesh denied the Great Nicobar Island Development Project has a military component, calling it "overwhelmingly a commercial enterprise." He criticized the Centre for allegedly portraying opponents as "soft on China" while pursuing a "Calibrated Capitulation."

articles.headline: SC flags lack of uniform excise laws, 'deceptive packaging' of liquor.
articles.summary: The Supreme Court sought responses from the Centre and States on widely differing excise laws, especially concerning the definition of a liquor "bottle." The court noted "deceptive packaging" of cheap alcohol, sold as "fruit juice," misleading consumers and raising health concerns.

articles.headline: Rules for new rural employment scheme to be issued soon.
articles.summary: The Union Ministry of Rural Development informed a parliamentary panel that rules for the new Viksit Bharat – Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission (Gramin) rural employment scheme will be released soon. The scheme is set to launch July 1, with 25 States already allocating funds.

articles.headline: 13-nation military exercise PRAGATI 2026 begins in Meghalaya.
articles.summary: A 13-nation military exercise, PRAGATI 2026, commenced at Umroi Military Station in Meghalaya. Soldiers from India and 12 friendly nations are participating to foster professional exchange and build closer military ties.

articles.headline: Nibe Ltd. conducts firing trials of indigenous long-range rockets.
articles.summary: Pune-based Nibe Ltd. successfully conducted firing trials of its indigenous Suryastra rockets (150 km and 300 km strike ranges) at the Integrated Test Range. This marks a significant milestone for India's private defence sector and defence preparedness.

articles.headline: NIA arrests Kolkata man for passing security info to Pakistan.
articles.summary: The NIA arrested Zafar Riaz alias Rizvi, a Kolkata resident, for allegedly passing confidential security information and providing Indian mobile OTPs to Pakistan intelligence officers (PIOs) for activating WhatsApp accounts used in espionage. He was previously convicted in an espionage case.

articles.headline: Pradhan reviews safeguards for NEET(UG) re-test, addresses misinformation.
articles.summary: Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan reviewed safeguards for the NEET(UG) 2026 re-test and met with tech companies to address misinformation on exam leaks. He emphasized safeguarding students and maintaining public confidence in competitive exams.

articles.headline: SRMIST, The Hindu to host webinar on agritech careers.
articles.summary: SRM Institute of Science and Technology (SRMIST) and The Hindu will co-host a webinar on 'Agri Tech Revolution: Engineering the Future Farming'. It will explore how technology is reshaping agriculture, creating career opportunities in agri-engineering, and improving productivity.

articles.headline: Australian LNG supplies to India expected to surge: High Commissioner Green.
articles.summary: Australian High Commissioner Philip Green predicts a surge in Australian LNG supplies to India, especially eastern India, via a crisis-free Indian Ocean route. He noted Australia's role in critical minerals like lithium and copper for India's clean energy sector.

articles.headline: Bharat Petroleum increases Russian crude oil share to 41%.
articles.summary: Bharat Petroleum's Director for Finance Vetsa Ramakrishna Gupta announced that Russian crude oil now constitutes 40-41% of its overall crude basket, up from 31% in Q4 FY25. This increase is due to wider availability in the spot market, with crude secured until July 2026.

articles.headline: NCDEX to launch India's first weather derivatives contract RAINMUMBAI.
articles.summary: The National Commodity and Derivatives Exchange (NCDEX) will launch RAINMUMBAI, India's first exchange-traded weather derivatives contract, on May 29. Developed with IIT Bombay, it allows businesses and farmers to hedge against monsoon variability using IMD rainfall data.

articles.headline: Putin, Xi hail 'unyielding' ties, sign agreements after Trump's China visit.
articles.summary: Presidents Xi Jinping and Vladimir Putin hailed China and Russia's "unyielding" ties in Beijing, extending their treaty of "friendly cooperation" and signing agreements on trade, media, and energy. Their meeting follows Donald Trump's China visit and strengthens their alliance amid global "unfavourable external factors."

articles.headline: Taiwan Travelogue wins International Booker Prize 2026.
articles.summary: Taiwanese author Yáng Shuāng-zi and translator Lin King won the International Booker Prize for "Taiwan Travelogue," a historical romance set in Japan-occupied Taiwan. It is the first novel in Mandarin Chinese to win the prestigious award for fiction translated into English.

articles.headline: Israel's Ben Gvir sparks row with video of bound Gaza flotilla activists.
articles.summary: Israel's far-right National Security Minister Itamar Ben Gvir posted a video showing detained Gaza-bound flotilla activists kneeling with tied hands and foreheads on the ground, sparking international condemnation. PM Netanyahu criticized the act as not aligned with "Israel's values."

articles.headline: WHO: Ebola risk high regionally, low worldwide.
articles.summary: The WHO stated the Democratic Republic of Congo’s Ebola outbreak risk is high at national and regional levels but low worldwide. While 51 cases are confirmed in DRC, the outbreak's true scale is larger, with nearly 600 suspected cases and 139 suspected deaths.

articles.headline: GT bowlers aim to boost top-two chances against CSK.
articles.summary: Gujarat Titans (GT) will face Chennai Super Kings (CSK) with a lethal pace attack, hoping to secure a win and improve their chances for Qualifier 1 in IPL 2026 playoffs. CSK, with a hit-and-miss campaign, needs an NRR-boosting victory and favorable external results.

articles.headline: Dhoni has sore thumb, not with CSK team: Coach Hussey.
articles.summary: CSK batting coach Michael Hussey confirmed M.S. Dhoni is not with the team due to a sore thumb for their IPL match against Gujarat Titans. Hussey added Dhoni would rejoin if CSK qualifies for the playoffs. GT remains focused on winning, not margin.

articles.headline: Dhairya Gogia shocks Tse Ka Chun in Asian junior squash.
articles.summary: India’s Dhairya Gogia upset Hong Kong’s 5/8 seed Tse Ka Chun 12-10, 10-12, 11-7, 11-7 in the first round of the boys’ U-13 section at the 33rd Asian junior squash championships in Panzhihua, China.

articles.headline: Mukund Sasikumar lone Indian in S.M. Krishna Memorial Open quarterfinals.
articles.summary: Mukund Sasikumar is the only Indian to reach the quarterfinals of the S.M. Krishna Memorial Open, defeating Japanese qualifier Taiyo Yamanaka 6-4, 7-6(1) in the round-of-16. Other Indian players, including wild card Kriish Tyagi, were eliminated.

articles.headline: Granit Xhaka named in Switzerland World Cup squad.
articles.summary: Veteran midfielder Granit Xhaka was named in Switzerland’s football World Cup squad, marking his seventh successive major international tournament. Switzerland reached the last-16 in the last three World Cups.

articles.headline: Punjab FC faces MCFC with ISL title on the line.
articles.summary: The 2025-26 Indian Super League season concludes with a drama-ridden final day, as four games kick off simultaneously. Punjab FC needs to win against Mumbai City FC and hope for other results to fall into place to claim the title, with five teams still in contention.

articles.headline: Madhav Tiwari shines for Delhi Capitals as rising all-rounder.
articles.summary: Madhav Tiwari, a 22-year-old all-rounder from Madhya Pradesh, has stepped up for Delhi Capitals in IPL 2026, taking two wickets apiece in two matches and contributing with the bat. He attracted attention playing for Bhopal Leopards in the MP T20 League.

articles.headline: Archery star Jyothi Surekha prepares for Olympic debut, embraces pressure.
articles.summary: India's most decorated archer, Jyothi Surekha Vennam, with over 90 medals, is preparing for compound archery's Olympic debut at the 2028 Los Angeles Games. The article details her journey, challenges, triple gold at the Asian Games, and her approach to sustained excellence and public expectation.

articles.headline: Sperm whale clicks show complex patterns akin to human speech.
articles.summary: A study in Proceedings of the Royal Society B reveals sperm whales' clicking vocalizations, called codas, vary in acoustic structure similar to human speech, using two distinct "vowel-like" click types. This suggests a sophisticated communication system with parallels to human phonology.

articles.headline: New cage-like crystal found in Trinity nuclear blast detritus.
articles.summary: Scientists discovered a rare, cage-like clathrate crystal in trinitite, the glassy crust formed by the 1945 Trinity nuclear test. This previously unknown calcium-copper-silicon crystal formed under extreme, fast-changing conditions of the blast and is metastable.

articles.headline: MF Industry Plans Own KRA via MFU to Slash KYC Costs by 40%
articles.summary: India's mutual fund industry is creating its own KYC Registration Agency (KRA) via Mutual Fund Utility (MFU) to cut industry-wide KYC costs by at least 40%. MFU is applying for a SEBI licence, aiming to disrupt CDSL & NSDL dominance, reduce burdens on asset managers, and make small investments more viable.

articles.headline: Ola Electric FY26 Revenue Halves, Q4 Loss Narrows Amid Competition
articles.summary: Ola Electric Mobility's FY26 revenue nearly halved to ₹2,253 crore, missing guidance. Losses narrowed to ₹1,833 crore. Rival Ather Energy surpassed Ola. In Q4 FY26, net loss was ₹500 crore (down 42.5% YoY, but up 2.7% sequentially), with revenue down 56.6% to ₹265 crore, amid operational challenges and competition.

articles.headline: Most APY Subscribers Opt for Lowest Pension; DFS Urges Higher Slabs
articles.summary: PFRDA data shows 87% of Atal Pension Yojana's (APY) over 90 million subscribers chose the minimum ₹1,000 pension, with only 8% opting for ₹5,000. DFS Secretary M Nagaraju urged banks to encourage subscribers to move to higher slabs as incomes grow. APY added 13.5 million subscribers in FY26, with assets over ₹54,000 crore.

articles.headline: India's Core Sector Growth Rises 1.7% in April
articles.summary: India's eight core infrastructure sectors grew 1.7% year-on-year in April, a marginal rise from 1.2% in March. This growth was driven by strong output in steel (+6.2%), cement (+9.4%), electricity (+4.1%), and coal. However, five industries, including crude oil (-3.9%) and natural gas (-4.3%), remained in contraction.

articles.headline: India, Italy Elevate Ties to Special Strategic Partnership at G7
articles.summary: India and Italy upgraded ties to a "Special Strategic Partnership" during PM Modi's G7 Summit visit, aiming for €20 billion annual trade by 2029. They firmed up a defence industrial roadmap, signed 10 MoUs, and discussed cooperation in critical minerals, technology, clean energy, skilled worker mobility, and IMEC connectivity. Leaders also stressed de-escalation in Ukraine and West Asia, and freedom of navigation.

articles.headline: "Melodi" Meme Boosts Melody Toffee Sales, Unrelated Stock Surges 5%
articles.summary: PM Modi gifting Melody toffees to Italian PM Meloni, fueling "Melodi" internet chemistry, caused sales surges & stockouts for Parle Products' Melody. The brand went viral, gaining significant earned media value. Separately, unrelated penny stock Parle Industries Ltd. saw its shares jump 5% due to mistaken identity by investors.

articles.headline: India to Send Tankers Through Hormuz for West Asia Supplies
articles.summary: India is preparing to send state-owned Shipping Corporation of India vessels through the Strait of Hormuz to load West Asia energy cargoes, a first since the Iran conflict halted shipping and caused price shocks. Final government/navy approval is pending.

articles.headline: SEBI Proposes Easing Third-Party Payments in Mutual Funds
articles.summary: SEBI is reviewing regulations to ease third-party payments in mutual funds, proposing to allow employer contributions for employee benefits via salary deductions and AMC commission payouts to distributors in MF units. This move, covering EPFO-registered companies, aims to ease transactions while retaining anti-money laundering safeguards. Public comments are due by June 10.

articles.headline: Meta Begins 8,000 Global Job Cuts for AI Efficiency Push
articles.summary: Meta Platforms Inc. initiated global job cuts of roughly 8,000 roles, impacting engineering and product teams, as part of a restructuring to cut costs and boost AI efficiency. Cuts started in Asia and will impact US staff, following 7,000 employees being reassigned to AI-focused teams.

articles.headline: NPCI BHIM to Expand to Feature Phones, Add New Financial Features
articles.summary: NPCI BHIM Services Ltd (NBSL) plans to double its market share and achieve profitability by expanding the BHIM app to feature phones via OEM collaborations, deepening financial inclusion. NBSL will also introduce new financial service features, partner with banks for product distribution, and integrate its tech into bank apps, after transaction volumes surged 301% in FY26.

articles.headline: SC Refuses Pause in KG Basin Gas Dispute; RIL Seeks Mediation
articles.summary: The Supreme Court refused to pause proceedings in the $1.55-$1.7 billion Krishna-Godavari (KG) Basin gas migration dispute between the Centre and an RIL-led consortium with foreign partners. RIL argues the arbitration is international, not domestic, and seeks conciliation/mediation. The Centre alleges "theft" of ONGC gas. The SC adjourned the hearing to February 2025 for a potential settlement.

articles.headline: Air India Cuts 40% International Capacity Amid Rising Costs
articles.summary: Air India is cutting domestic (up to 5%) and international (nearly 40%) capacity this summer, operating fewer flights than last year. This pushes the Tata Group carrier into "survival mode" due to a depleted fleet, surging jet fuel costs, and war-related airspace closures, while rivals IndiGo and Akasa Air are expanding.

articles.headline: Rupee Hits Lifetime Low of 96.86 Against Dollar Amid Crisis
articles.summary: The Indian rupee depreciated 16 paise, closing at a new lifetime low of 96.86 against the US dollar for the ninth consecutive session. Elevated global crude prices due to the West Asia crisis, capital outflows, widening trade deficits, a strong dollar, and surging US treasury yields are fueling inflation worries and weakening the currency.

articles.headline: Grasim Industries Q4 Net Profit Rises 28% to Record Revenue
articles.summary: Aditya Birla Group's Grasim Industries reported a 27.9% rise in Q4 FY26 consolidated net profit to ₹3,802.23 crore, driven by strong performance and better realizations in building materials, financial services, and cellulosic fibres. Revenue from operations surged 15.43% to a "highest-ever" ₹51,101.11 crore, with building materials revenue alone hitting a record ₹30,042 crore.

articles.headline: RBI to Inject $5 Billion Via Dollar/Rupee Swap Auction May 26
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India will conduct a $5 billion dollar/rupee buy/sell swap auction on May 26 for a three-year tenor. This move, following a liquidity review, aims to inject long-term liquidity into the banking system and defend the rapidly weakening rupee, which has depreciated over 6% since the Iran war began amid global uncertainties.

articles.headline: SC Reserves Verdict on Yes Bank AT1 Bond Write-Off Dispute
articles.summary: The Supreme Court reserved judgment on the ₹8,415 crore write-off of Yes Bank's AT1 bonds after hearings. The Centre defended the write-down, citing the bank's 2020 survival, RBI circulars, and SBI's ₹8,000 crore infusion, despite the SC pulling up the finance ministry. Bondholders challenged it as an "extreme" step violating loss hierarchy. The case sets a precedent for bank resolution.

articles.headline: Jubilant FoodWorks Q4 Profit Jumps 67%; Domino's Growth Muted
articles.summary: Jubilant FoodWorks, Domino's India operator, reported a 66-67% rise in Q4 net profit to ₹79.79-₹82.4 crore. Consolidated revenue grew 19.3% to ₹2,499-₹2,499.5 crore, driven by steady orders and new stores. Delivery volumes grew in high teens, but Domino's India saw muted like-for-like growth (0.2%) due to temporary March LPG supply issues, now normalized.

articles.headline: MP CM Seeks CBI Probe into Bhopal Dowry Death Case
articles.summary: Madhya Pradesh Chief Minister Mohan Yadav assured a CBI probe into the dowry death of 32/33-year-old Twisha Sharma, found hanging last week. Her family alleged dowry harassment and investigation lapses. Her husband and mother were booked under BNS sections 80 and 85, after a district court rejected a plea for a second post-mortem.

articles.headline: Congress Joins Tamil Nadu Cabinet After 59 Years; Two MLAs Sworn In
articles.summary: In a historic move, Congress will join Tamil Nadu's TVK/DMK-led government after 59 years, forming the state's first coalition. Two Congress MLAs, S. Rajesh Kumar (Tourism) and P. Viswanathan (Higher Education), will take oath as ministers. This post-poll alliance strengthens the DMK-Congress ties after the TVK fell short of a majority.

articles.headline: Rahul Gandhi Calls PM Modi, Shah "Traitors"; BJP Slams Remarks
articles.summary: Rahul Gandhi called PM Modi, Amit Shah, and RSS "traitors," accusing them of attacking the Constitution, selling national interests to industrialists, and snatching marginalized sections' reservation. Speaking in Amethi, he also warned of an economic crisis and criticized Modi's foreign travel. The BJP slammed his remarks as frustrated and an insult to the electorate.

articles.headline: Xi, Putin Condemn US Missile Defense & Nuclear Policy at Summit
articles.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping and Russian President Vladimir Putin, at a joint summit, condemned US President Donald Trump’s Golden Dome missile defence plans and "irresponsible" nuclear policy. They stated Trump's plan threatens global strategic stability and criticized Washington over the expiry of a treaty restricting US and Russian nuclear arsenals. No gas pipeline deal was announced.

articles.headline: Arsenal Ends 22-Year Drought, Wins Premier League Title
articles.summary: Arsenal ended their 22-year Premier League title drought, securing their 14th English title after Manchester City's 1-1 draw at Bournemouth. Manager Mikel Arteta transformed the club from 2021, building a strong team with key contributions from Declan Rice, Bukayo Saka, and David Raya, sparking global celebrations for their first title since 2004.

articles.headline: Trump to Speak with Taiwan President; Lai Seeks Continued US Arms
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump announced he would speak with Taiwan’s President Lai Ching-te, an unprecedented move since 1979 that could strain US-China ties. Trump has approved record weapon sales to Taiwan, and Lai Ching-te hopes to continue US arms purchases, calling them essential for peace amid growing pressure from China.

articles.headline: SC Extends Bengaluru Civic Polls Deadline to August 31, Warns Against Delay
articles.summary: The Supreme Court extended the deadline for Bengaluru civic body (BBMP) elections by two months to August 31, citing the Karnataka government's requests due to Census work, electoral roll revision, and budget duties. The SC, however, expressed concern over "delaying tactics" and declared this the "last chance," with no further extensions for BBMP and GBA polls.

articles.headline: AxisCades to Invest ₹6,000 Cr for Aerospace-Defence Hub in Karnataka
articles.summary: Bengaluru-based AxisCades Aerospace Infrastructure Pvt Ltd will invest ₹6,000 crore to establish a 200-acre integrated hub near Devanahalli, Karnataka. The facility will focus on manufacturing, testing, maintenance, and R&D for aerospace, defence, semiconductor, and AI sectors. The project, including a 1.8-km runway, is expected to create 20,000 direct and 54,000 indirect jobs.

articles.headline: Kerala Scraps SilverLine Rail Project; Land to be De-Notified
articles.summary: The Kerala Cabinet, led by CM V D Satheesan, scrapped the previous LDF government’s controversial 530-km K-Rail (SilverLine) semi-high-speed railway project, citing environmental and economic unviability. All acquired land will be de-notified, and criminal cases against protesters will be recommended for withdrawal, addressing significant public opposition.

articles.headline: Kerala's Milma to Hike Milk Price by ₹4/Litre from June 1
articles.summary: Kerala's Milma will increase milk prices by ₹4 per litre from June 1, with ₹3.35 of the hike going to dairy farmers. Approved by the Left government, this move addresses steep production costs, low farmer returns, and drought conditions. The price for 500ml toned milk will rise to ₹28.

articles.headline: Andhra Approves Adani 2,250MW Storage Project, RIL AI Data Centre
articles.summary: Andhra Pradesh approved Adani Hydro Energy Eleven Ltd's 2,250 MW Gandikota-2 pumped storage project in YSR Kadapa district under its Clean Energy Policy. Critical for India's energy future, it's due in 72 months. The state also approved over 800 acres for a ₹1 trillion RIL AI data centre with a cable landing station.

articles.headline: Over 500 Bonded Labourers, Including Minors, Rescued in Telangana
articles.summary: In large-scale raids, 518-529 bonded labourers, including 226 minors (9 infants) from 135 families, were rescued from brick kilns in Telangana's Nizamabad district. The operation was initiated after an escapee filed a complaint. Most rescued individuals were shifted to Nellore, Andhra Pradesh, awaiting rehabilitation assistance.

articles.headline: India, South Korea Deepen Defence Cooperation; Sign Pacts
articles.summary: India and South Korea signed agreements to deepen defence cooperation in cybersecurity, training, UN peacekeeping, maritime security, emerging technologies, military exchanges, logistics, and production. Defence Minister Rajnath Singh and his Korean counterpart discussed expanding collaboration, reflecting convergence of strategic visions in the Indo-Pacific, during a Seoul round table.

articles.headline: Centre Bans New Hydel Projects in Upper Ganga Basin
articles.summary: The Centre informed the Supreme Court it will permit no new hydroelectric projects in the upper Ganga's Alaknanda-Bhagirathi basin in Uttarakhand, rejecting 28 proposals. Seven existing or advanced-stage projects will proceed. This decision prioritizes ecological sensitivity and addresses environmental impact and seismic fragility concerns, following past disaster warnings.
