articles.headline: CBI Books Retired Navy Captain for Disproportionate Assets
articles.summary: The CBI registered a disproportionate assets case against retired Indian Navy Captain Raminder Singh Wadhwa, alleging he amassed over ₹3 crore in assets between 2010-2020. Wadhwa, who retired in May 2024, held sensitive postings including at the National Security Council Secretariat.

articles.headline: West Bengal Hands Over Land to BSF for Border Fencing
articles.summary: West Bengal government has handed over 27 km (75 acres) of land to the BSF for fencing along the Indo-Bangladesh border, a move expected to strengthen security and curb illegal infiltration and smuggling. CM Suvendu Adhikari also ordered police to detain and deport illegal Bangladeshi immigrants, marking a shift from previous government policies.

articles.headline: Calcutta HC Hears Pleas for Eid Animal Slaughter Exemption
articles.summary: The Calcutta High Court is hearing petitions seeking exemption under Section 12 of the Animal Slaughter Control Act, 1950, for Eid al-Adha. This follows new BJP government guidelines barring animal slaughter without a "fit certificate" and prohibiting it in open public places. A division bench listed the pleas for Thursday.

articles.headline: Delhi HC Seeks Spy Film Policy After "Dhurandhar" Concerns
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court asked the Centre and CBFC to consider a plea for a specific policy on spy-thriller films. The petition argues that scenes in “Dhurandhar: The Revenge” could reveal sensitive national security information, violate the Official Secrets Act, and compromise undercover agents’ safety, even if fictional.

articles.headline: Iran Warns of Regional War if US Attacks Resume
articles.summary: Iran warned that a renewed war would spread "beyond the region" if the US attacks again, after President Donald Trump threatened strikes if no peace deal is reached, following an April 8 ceasefire. Iran’s chief negotiator said the US wants to restart the Middle East war. Iran is examining a new US proposal, having repeated previous demands for Strait of Hormuz control and war compensation.

articles.headline: James Murdoch Acquires Three Major Vox Media Divisions
articles.summary: James Murdoch's Lupa Systems is acquiring three major divisions of Vox Media, including New York Magazine and its podcast network, reaching tens of millions. The $300M+ deal, excluding brands like Eater and The Verge, gives Murdoch control of recognizable media brands and a sizable podcast business, as digital publishers seek new growth avenues.

articles.headline: KKR Beats Mumbai Indians, Stays Alive in IPL Playoff Race
articles.summary: Kolkata Knight Riders (KKR) defeated Mumbai Indians by four wickets at Eden Gardens in IPL 2026, chasing a target of 147-148 runs. Manish Pandey (45) and Rovman Powell (40) guided KKR to victory with seven balls to spare, keeping their playoff hopes alive with 13 points and one match remaining.

articles.headline: BCCI Announces 2026-27 Domestic Cricket Schedule
articles.summary: The BCCI announced its 2026-27 domestic season schedule: Duleep Trophy kicks off on August 23, followed by the Ranji Trophy on October 11 (in two phases). The season concludes March 28 with the Women's Inter-Zonal Multi-Day tournament. The U-23 limited-overs tournament is now T20, and BCCI will organize 1788 games.

articles.headline: Taijul Islam Guides Bangladesh to Historic Test Series Sweep vs Pakistan
articles.summary: Spinner Taijul Islam took six wickets (6/120) to lead Bangladesh to a 78-run win over Pakistan in the second Test at Sylhet, clinching a historic 2-0 series sweep. Pakistan was bowled out for 328 chasing 437, despite Mohammad Rizwan's 94. This marks Bangladesh's first Test series win at home against Pakistan and second successive sweep.

articles.headline: India, South Korea to Jointly Develop Advanced Defense Technologies
articles.summary: India and South Korea have agreed to collaborate on developing advanced defense technologies, including laser weapons, mobile air defense platforms, and directed-energy weapon systems. Defence Minister Rajnath Singh, in Seoul, emphasized leveraging India's scale and South Korea's technology to create future-ready systems for both nations, global export, and regional security.

articles.headline: Ola Electric Narrows Q4 Loss to ₹500 Crore Despite Revenue Drop
articles.summary: Ola Electric narrowed its Q4 FY26 net loss by 42-42.5% to ₹500 crore (from ₹870 crore), despite a 56% revenue decline to ₹265 crore. Attributing this to higher scooter sales and operational efficiency, the company, in a "year of reset" focusing on service recovery and cost rationalization, aims for profitability via its IPO, expecting Q1 FY27 orders to double.

articles.headline: LSG Coach Langer Praises Teen Batting Sensation Sooryavanshi
articles.summary: Lucknow Super Giants coach Justin Langer lauded 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi for his "breathtaking" IPL batting, including a 38-ball 93 against LSG (or rapid fifty against Rajasthan Royals). Langer, comparing his power-hitting to greats, predicts a bright international future for the young Orange Cap holder who has "lit up world cricket."

articles.headline: Samvardhana Motherson Q4 Net Profit Jumps 46%, Plans ₹5000 Cr NCDs
articles.summary: Samvardhana Motherson International (SAMIL) reported a 46% Y-o-Y rise in Q4 FY26 consolidated net profit, reaching ₹1,497-₹1,561.56 crore due to robust business performance. Total revenue increased 8.4% to ₹34,309.31 crore, and the board approved raising ₹5,000 crore via non-convertible debentures.

articles.headline: Apollo Hospitals Q4 Net Profit Jumps 36%, Plans Cloudnine Merger
articles.summary: Apollo Hospitals Enterprises (AHEL) reported a 36% rise in Q4 FY26 consolidated net profit to ₹529 crore, with revenue up 18% to ₹6,605 crore. Full-year FY26 net profit grew 34% to ₹1,942 crore (revenue ₹25,229 crore), driven by strong demand. The board also approved a ₹1,550 crore deal to combine Apollo Cradle & Fertility with Cloudnine.

articles.headline: US Secretary Rubio to Visit India; Discuss Trade, Defence, Energy Security
articles.summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio will make his first visit to India from May 23-26 to discuss bilateral cooperation in trade, defence, and energy security, especially amidst the West Asia crisis and US-Iran war impacting India's energy. Talks will also cover the proposed India-US bilateral trade agreement and Indo-Pacific security.

articles.headline: SEBI's Revised Methodology Boosts India's FY25 Savings Ratio
articles.summary: SEBI's revised methodology for calculating household savings via securities markets boosted India's FY25 gross savings-to-GDP ratio by 47 bps to 34.94% (from 34.47%). Net household financial savings also improved to 7.10% of GDP. The new framework uses transaction-level data for comprehensive coverage of market investments.

articles.headline: Gold Edges Up as West Asia Hopes Ease Yields, Oil Prices
articles.summary: Gold prices rose 0.4% to $4,499.72 per ounce as hopes for West Asia conflict resolution eased Iran conflict fears, pressing oil markets and relieving inflation worries. US Treasury yields also eased from recent highs, reducing the opportunity cost of holding non-yielding bullion. Investors are pricing a 48.6% chance of a Fed rate hike in December.

articles.headline: Navi Mumbai Airport UDF Set Higher, Will Increase Until FY30
articles.summary: India's AERA set User Development Fees (UDF) for Navi Mumbai airport. For FY27, existing UDFs of ₹620 (domestic departing) and ₹1,225 (international departing) are retained, but are already 3.5 times higher than Mumbai airport for domestic passengers. These levies will progressively increase annually from FY28 to FY30 to support greenfield investment recovery, leading to higher airfares.

articles.headline: India's Core Sector Growth Rises 1.7% in April
articles.summary: India's eight core sectors saw marginal growth of 1.7% in April 2026-27, up from 1.2% in March. This was driven by stronger steel (+6.2%) and electricity (+4.1%) growth and narrowing fertilizer contraction, despite five sectors (like crude oil) contracting and ongoing supply-side issues from the West Asia crisis.

articles.headline: IMF Advises Targeted Relief Over Blanket Subsidies for Price Shocks
articles.summary: The IMF advised governments against broad subsidies for West Asia conflict price shocks, citing fiscal strain. Instead, it suggested "temporary, targeted, timely, and tailored fiscal measures" like cash transfers and deferrals, allowing energy prices to reflect international costs. Generalized subsidies should be reserved for truly exceptional shocks.

articles.headline: CAG Murthy Urges Stronger Ties with CCI to Curb Price Fixing
articles.summary: CAG Sanjay Murthy urged stronger partnership between CAG and the Competition Commission of India (CCI), citing their complementary roles in protecting public finances and market integrity. He emphasized curbing price fixing and bid rigging in government procurement, which cause measurable loss. CCI Chairperson Ravneet Kaur highlighted AI as a key regulatory challenge.

articles.headline: India, US Industry Clash at USTR Section 301 Hearings Over Tariffs
articles.summary: At USTR's Section 301 hearings, US industry groups pushed for punitive tariffs on Indian steel and ferroalloys, alleging state-backed overcapacity and unfair subsidies. Indian officials, represented by James Nedumpara, countered that India's manufacturing expansion is market-driven, demand-led, and compliant with global trade rules, asserting exports do not warrant tariffs.

articles.headline: Irdai Withholds Variable Pay of Insurance CEOs Over Unmet Targets
articles.summary: Irdai withheld variable pay for CEOs of insurance companies failing to meet "expense of management" (EoM) targets or adhere to committed glide paths in FY25-FY26. Insurers who breached FY23 EoM limits had submitted board-approved compliance plans for FY26; Irdai now applies stricter scrutiny for significant deviations.

articles.headline: SEBI Proposes Part Salary Payouts in Mutual Fund Units
articles.summary: SEBI proposes allowing Indian companies to pay a portion of employee salaries and MFs to pay distributor commissions in mutual fund units. This aims to foster disciplined investment and long-term savings, with safeguards including KYC and direct credit of redemptions to beneficiary accounts.

articles.headline: RBI Allows Lenders to Disable Financed Mobiles for Default
articles.summary: RBI's draft guidelines allow banks/NBFCs to disable or restrict financed mobile devices/tablets for loan default, provided contracts explicitly permit. Strict notice periods apply (60 days overdue + 21 days to cure, then 7 days notice). Essential functions must remain active, data access is prohibited, and wrongful restriction incurs a ₹250/hour penalty.
